creator: wxs@FreeBSD.org
text:
I this this patch should be rejected. This is a local change that should
be made to the port. What are the side effects of increasing the number
of bpf devices? What's going to stop someone from wanting to increase
the number in the future? At some point 256 will not be enough and we
will have set the precedent that increasing this limit is acceptable.
I'm not sure this is a good idea.

There has been some work in the recent past that would allow for local
patches to be integrated into the ports framework. I intend to see about
getting that work cleaned up and committed so that this kind of thing
becomes trivial to implement locally.

-- WXS
